english: gree of an adj. or adv., and esp. a gradual growing or increase, often with termin. or la: je maṅ gro (je mang gro) (they) go on increasing or multiplying in number Mil.; je gsál-du soṅ (je gsal du song) it has become more and more clear or evident Thgr.; gen. repeated: je nye je nye soṅ-ste (je nye je nye song ste) going nearer and nearer Mil.; je čuṅ je čuṅ-la soṅ (je chung je chung la song), also je čuṅ je nyuṅ (je chung je nyung) Mil. less and less; sometimes also for the superlative degree, Cs.: je dáṅ-po (je dang po) the very first, also Lex. -- 3. jé-żig (je zhig) a little while, = ré-żig (re zhig) Lex. -- 4. Bhar. 14, Schf.: 'an adhortative particle, often connected with a vocative'; Sch. has: je k´yod (je khyod) 'now you, you first!' -- 5. = dbyaṅs (dbyangs) Lex.
1. num. fig.: 127. -- 2. v. the following word.

1. C. the elder brother, also *jo-jo* (*jo jo*) and *á-jo* (*a jo*) (the latter also in W.), resp. jo-légs (jo legs). -- 2. lord, master, esp. nobleman, grandee, W. *jo* (*jo*), yar-luṅ jó-bo (yar lung jo bo) Glr. the lord of the manor of Yarlung; *ti-nán jo* (*ti nan jo*) W. the nobleman of Tinan; jo-jo miṅ-po (jo jo ming po) my noble brothers (says a princess) Glr.; in C. used as honorary title for noblemen and priests, in W. also for noble Mussulmans; in ancient times for certain divine persons, and idols, particularly for two, famous in history: jó-bo mi-skyod-rdó-rje (jo bo mi skyod rdo rje), and tsan-dán-gyi jó-bo (tsan dan gyi jo bo), also jó-bo śá-kya (jo bo sha kya), jó-bo rin-po-čé (jo bo rin po che) v. Glr.

1. mistress, the female head of a household, a woman that governs as mistress of her servants Dzl. -- 2. lady, esp. a cloistress, nun Mil.; in W. frq. -- 3. goddess (cf. sub jo-bo 2 (jo bo 2)), jo-mo sgrol-ma (jo mo sgrol ma) the goddess Dolma Glr. -- 4. p. n. jo-mo-lha-ri (jo mo lha ri) one of the highest mountain summits in West-Bhotan, usually called 'Chumulhari'; jo-mo-k´a-nag (jo mo kha nag) another summit in southern Tibet.

imp. mjol (mjol), 1. to meet c. daṅ (dang), = p´rád-pa (phrad pa), without any respect to rank, Mil. often. More frq. 2. resp.: to obtain access to an honoured person; żal-dṅós-su mjál-bar yod (zhal dngos su mjal bar yod) he (the incarnated Buddha) may personally be seen and spoken to Glr.; to wait on, to pay one's respects to a person, yab daṅ mjál tšál-lo (yab dang mjal tshal lo) I will pay a visit to my father Dzl.; p´yis myúr-du mjál-du yoṅ (phyis myur du mjal du yong) I shall take the liberty of soon coming back Mil.; rgyál-bai sku daṅ t´a-mal mjál (rgyal bai sku dang tha mal mjal) to thee, Buddha, my own humble self approaches (says a prince to his father who appears to be an incarnated Buddha) Glr.; mjál-bar żú-ba (mjal bar zhu ba) to ask for an audience Glr.; mjál-du mi btub (mjal du mi btub) (they) cannot get in, cannot obtain admittance Pth.; *jal-čág čó-če* (*jal cag co ce*) (or *čag-jal* (*cag jal*) Cs.) W. to salute, to exchange compliments on meeting; mjál-p´rád-byéd-pa (mjal phrad byed pa) = p´rád-pa (phrad pa); used also of a king and his ministers: mjál-p´rád daṅ dgá-bai gtam máṅ-po mdzad (mjal phrad dang dga' bai gtam mang po mdzad) (they) exchanged many compliments and expressions of joy Pth.; to visit or pay one's respects to holy places, as pilgrims do, to go on a pilgrimage, also żal mjál-ba (zhal mjal ba) Mil.; gnas mjál-ba (gnas mjal ba) id., gnas-mjál-pa (gnas mjal pa) partic., a pilgrim, palmer; di mjól żig (di mjol zhig) do make your pilgrimage to this place. -- 3. to understand, comprehend, Zam.: 'gó-bai mjál-ba ('go bai mjal ba) གོ་བ་; don mjál-ba (don mjal ba) to understand the sense Mil., yet cf. jál-ba (jal ba) 3. -- 4 often erron. for jál-ba (jal ba). Comp. mjál-k´a (mjal kha) audience, access, admittance, mjál-k´a gtóṅ-ba (mjal kha gtong ba), or gnáṅ-ba (gnang ba) to give audience, gégs-pa (gegs pa) to refuse it Mil. -- mjál-dár (mjal dar) = k´a-btágs (kha btags). -- mjál-sna-pa (mjal sna pa) an usher, master of ceremonies Cs. -- mjál-p´yág (mjal phyag) salutation. -- mjál-máṅs (mjal mangs) a visit paid by many together, a grand reception Cs.
1. = jiṅ-pa (jing pa). -- 2. rtswa-mjiṅ (rtswa mjing) meadow Bhar. 82, Schf.

what is behind, hind part, e.g. of the body, resp. sku-mjúg (sku mjug), posteriors, back-side, tail, often also mjúg-ma (mjug ma); mjug skór-ba (mjug skor ba) col. to turn one's back (on another); mjúg-ma sgril-ba (mjug ma sgril ba) to wag the tail; fig.: the further progress and final issue of an affair, the consequences = rjes (rjes), opp. to dṅos-gżi (dngos gzhi) the thing itself, and to sṅón-gro (sngon gro) the preparations Thgy.; the lower end or extremity, e.g. of a bench, a stick, a river (= mouth), of a procession, train etc.; with regard to time: the end, zlá-ba brgyád-pai mjúg-la (zla ba brgyad pai mjug la), at the end of the eighth month;
